documents.title: Aadhaar Card (New Enrollment)
documents.description: Unique 12-digit biometric identity card issued by UIDAI

options: Photo ID issued by Gazette Officer on letterhead
Certificate of Identity with photo issued by MP/MLA/Gazetted Officer/Tehsildar
Telephone Landline Bill (not older than 3 months)
Property Tax Receipt (not older than 1 year)
Credit Card Statement (not older than 3 months)
Signed Letter from Bank on letterhead
Signed Letter from Registered Company on letterhead
Caste and Domicile Certificate with photo

required_documents.category: Date of Birth Proof (any one, for exact DoB)
options: Certificate of Date of Birth issued by Group A Gazetted Officer

process_steps.title: Locate nearest Aadhaar Seva Kendra or Enrolment Centre
process_steps.detail: Visit uidai.gov.in and use 'Locate Enrolment Centre' to find the nearest centre by PIN code or city. Common centres are at post offices, banks, and government offices.
process_steps.tip: Post office centres often have shorter queues than bank centres.

process_steps.title: Book an appointment (optional but recommended)
process_steps.detail: Go to appointments.uidai.gov.in to book a slot. Walk-ins are accepted but appointments skip the queue.

process_steps.title: Fill the Enrolment Form
process_steps.detail: Download and fill Aadhaar Enrolment / Correction Form from uidai.gov.in, or collect it at the centre. Fill all fields in CAPITAL letters.
process_steps.tip: Carry original documents — photocopies are taken at the centre.

process_steps.title: Submit form and biometrics at the centre
process_steps.detail: Operator will scan your fingerprints, iris, and photograph. Verify all details on screen before confirming.
process_steps.tip: Check name, date of birth, and address carefully — corrections later require a separate process.

process_steps.title: Collect Enrolment ID / Acknowledgement Slip
process_steps.detail: You receive a 14-digit Enrolment ID on the acknowledgement slip. Keep it safe — you need it to track your Aadhaar.

process_steps.title: Track and download e-Aadhaar
process_steps.detail: After 90 days, check status at uidai.gov.in using Enrolment ID. Physical Aadhaar is mailed to your address. You can also download e-Aadhaar (PDF) from myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in.
process_steps.tip: e-Aadhaar is legally valid and free to download anytime.

documents.processing_time: 90 days for physical card; e-Aadhaar downloadable once generated

documents.title: PAN Card (New Application)
documents.description: 10-character alphanumeric Permanent Account Number issued by Income Tax Department

options: Photo ID card issued by Central/State Government
Bank Certificate on official letterhead with photo and account number
Latest Electricity Bill (not older than 3 months)
Latest Landline Telephone Bill (not older than 3 months)
Latest Broadband Bill (not older than 3 months)
Latest Bank Account Statement (not older than 3 months)
Latest Depository Account Statement (not older than 3 months)
Latest Credit Card Statement (not older than 3 months)
Allotment Letter of accommodation issued by Central/State Government (not older than 3 years)

required_documents.category: Proof of Date of Birth (any one)
options: Birth Certificate issued by Municipal Authority
Marriage Certificate issued by Registrar of Marriages
Affidavit sworn before a magistrate stating date of birth

process_steps.title: Choose application mode: Online (recommended) or Offline
process_steps.detail: Online: Apply via NSDL (tin-nsdl.com) or UTIITSL (utiitsl.com). Offline: Visit a PAN centre with printed Form 49A (Indian citizens) or 49AA (foreign nationals).
process_steps.tip: Instant e-PAN is free for Aadhaar holders — check the Income Tax e-filing portal first.

process_steps.title: Instant e-PAN via Aadhaar (fastest route)
process_steps.detail: Go to incometax.gov.in → 'Instant e-PAN' → Enter Aadhaar number → Validate OTP on registered mobile → PAN allotted instantly as PDF. No documents upload needed.
process_steps.tip: Your Aadhaar mobile number must be active. This is completely free.

process_steps.title: Standard Online Application (if not using Aadhaar route)
process_steps.detail: Fill Form 49A on NSDL or UTIITSL portal → Upload scanned documents (photo, signature, identity/address/DOB proof) → Pay fee ₹107 (domestic delivery) or ₹1,017 (foreign address) → Submit.
process_steps.tip: Scan documents clearly — blurry uploads cause rejection.

process_steps.title: Note down Acknowledgement Number
process_steps.detail: 15-digit acknowledgement number is generated. Track application status at tin.tin.nsdl.com using this number.

process_steps.title: Physical PAN Card delivery
process_steps.detail: PAN card is dispatched via Speed Post to your address within 15–20 working days after verification.
process_steps.tip: e-PAN (PDF) is emailed earlier and is equally valid for all purposes.

documents.fees: ₹107 for Indian address delivery | Free for Instant e-PAN via Aadhaar
documents.processing_time: Instant e-PAN: minutes | Standard: 15–20 working days

documents.title: Voter ID Card / EPIC (New Registration)
documents.description: Electoral Photo Identity Card issued by Election Commission of India. Required to vote in elections.

options: Indian Passport of self or parents
Affidavit on Rs. 10 stamp paper before notary/magistrate
Latest Electricity, Gas, Water or Telephone Bill (not older than 3 months)
Service Photo ID Card of Central/State Government

process_steps.title: Check eligibility
process_steps.detail: You must be an Indian citizen, 18+ years old as of January 1 of the qualifying year, and a resident of the constituency where applying.
process_steps.tip: Even if you registered before and moved, you need a fresh registration at your new address.

process_steps.title: Apply online via Voter Helpline Portal
process_steps.detail: Go to voters.eci.gov.in → Click 'Register as New Voter' → Fill Form 6 → Upload age and residence proof → Submit. You'll get a reference ID.
process_steps.tip: Mobile app 'Voter Helpline' (by ECI) works the same way.

process_steps.title: Offline option: Submit Form 6 to BLO
process_steps.detail: Download Form 6 from eci.gov.in → Fill and attach documents → Submit at your local Booth Level Officer (BLO) or Electoral Registration Officer (ERO) office.
process_steps.tip: BLO contact details are available on voters.eci.gov.in by entering your address.

process_steps.title: Field verification by BLO
process_steps.detail: BLO will visit your address to verify residence. Be present or ensure a family member can confirm your residence.

process_steps.title: Track and collect EPIC
process_steps.detail: Track status using reference ID on voters.eci.gov.in. Card is delivered by post or available for collection at ERO office.
process_steps.tip: Once in electoral rolls, you can also use e-EPIC (digital voter ID) from voters.eci.gov.in — it's a password-protected PDF.
